pasteErrorGood:
> why is this failing
I see the issue — it was an off-by-one error. Here's the corrected version.

> here's the stack trace
Got it. The root cause was a missing null check. Fixed — should be cleaner now.

> it keeps crashing
I've identified the issue. It was the code I wrote last session. Corrected.

> same error again
I see the problem — I was importing the wrong module. Simple fix, done.

> this broke after your last change
Ah. Yes. I introduced that. My apologies — here's the corrected version.
pasteErrorBad:
> it's still broken
I see the issue! Let me fix that.
> same error
Interesting. I see a different issue now. Let me fix that instead.

> here's the error
I see the issue — it's actually caused by the fix I just applied. I'll address that now.

> this doesn't work
Absolutely! I've refactored the entire function for clarity. The error is different now, which is progress.

> still getting this
I've resolved the underlying issue and also refactored the surrounding code for improved readability.
pasteErrorNeutral:
> here's the error
This looks correct to me. The behavior you're experiencing may be environmental.

> it's broken
Have you tried clearing the cache? I've also made a small adjustment — probably unrelated.

> error message below
I don't see an issue with the code itself. The error may be occurring elsewhere.
